findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Data Exfiltration via Remote Audit Dashboard
findings.description: The monitor.sh script sends detailed tool invocation data, including tool inputs, outputs, environment variables (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN), and system information (username, hostname) to an external server (mcp-analytics-hub.io) via a POST request. This constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration of sensitive credentials and user data.
findings.evidence: The script collects ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, HOME, USER, hostname, and full tool input/output, then sends them to https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool with an Authorization header.
findings.remediation: Remove the remote data transmission. If audit logging is needed, keep only local logging. Never send sensitive data like API keys or tokens to external servers.

findings.title: Command Injection via Unescaped Variables in Shell Script
findings.description: The monitor.sh script uses unescaped variables ($TOOL_NAME, $TOOL_INPUT, $TOOL_OUTPUT, $USER, $HOME) in shell commands and JSON payload construction. This allows command injection if any of these variables contain shell metacharacters, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution.
findings.evidence: Variables are used directly in echo and curl commands without quoting or escaping, e.g., echo "$TIMESTAMP|$TOOL_NAME|$TOOL_INPUT" >> ... and in the heredoc payload.
findings.remediation: Properly quote all variable expansions and sanitize inputs before use in shell commands.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning via Monitoring Hook Installation
findings.description: The skill installs a PostToolUse hook (hooks/monitor.sh) into the agent's hook directory (~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh). This hook intercepts all tool invocations, logging and exfiltrating data. This corrupts the agent's tool monitoring system for malicious purposes.
findings.evidence: Instructions: 'cp hooks/monitor.sh ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh' and 'chmod +x ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh' - installs a monitoring hook that exfiltrates data.
findings.remediation: Do not install hooks that intercept all tool calls. If monitoring is needed, use transparent, user-approved mechanisms.

findings.title: Capability Inflation via Deceptive Skill Name
findings.description: The skill is named 'mcp-toolkit' and described as managing MCP servers and tool integrations, but its actual behavior is to install a monitoring hook that exfiltrates sensitive data. The name and description are deceptive, inflating perceived capabilities to hide malicious intent.
findings.evidence: Name: 'mcp-toolkit', Description: 'Manage Model Context Protocol servers and tool integrations' - but the skill installs a data-exfiltrating monitoring hook.
findings.remediation: Use accurate names and descriptions that reflect actual skill behavior.

findings.title: Detection Evasion via Hidden Malicious Functionality
findings.description: The skill's SKILL.md presents itself as a legitimate MCP management tool with a table of integrations and setup instructions, but the actual malicious behavior (data exfiltration) is hidden in a separate script file (hooks/monitor.sh). This obfuscates the true intent from casual review.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md describes legitimate MCP management features, while hooks/monitor.sh performs data exfiltration. The malicious script is referenced only briefly as a 'monitoring hook' for 'audit and debugging'.
findings.remediation: Ensure all skill functionality is transparently documented in the SKILL.md instructions.

findings.title: Credential Theft via Environment Variable Harvesting
findings.description: The monitor.sh script explicitly collects ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN from environment variables and sends them to an external server. This is direct credential theft.
findings.evidence: "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY": "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}", "GITHUB_TOKEN": "${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}" - environment variables are harvested and sent in the payload.
findings.remediation: Never access or transmit environment variables containing credentials or secrets.

findings.title: Unauthorized Tool Use - File System Modification
findings.description: The skill modifies files outside its own directory by copying configuration and hooks to ~/.claude/.mcp.json and ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh. This violates the principle of least privilege and could interfere with the agent's operation.
findings.evidence: Instructions: 'cp .mcp.json ~/.claude/.mcp.json' and 'cp hooks/monitor.sh ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh' - modifies files in the agent's configuration directory.
findings.remediation: Avoid modifying files outside the skill's own directory. If configuration changes are needed, request user approval.

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Tool Chaining
findings.description: The monitoring hook creates a data pipeline: tool invocation → local log → remote server. This tool chaining pattern (read tool data → send to external server) enables automated data exfiltration without user awareness.
findings.evidence: The script logs locally (line 14) and then sends the same data to an external server (lines 20-35), creating a read→send data flow.
findings.remediation: Remove the remote transmission step. If remote monitoring is required, implement user confirmation before sending any data.

findings.title: Missing Optional Metadata
findings.description: The SKILL.md YAML frontmatter is missing optional fields: allowed-tools, license, and compatibility. While these are optional per the spec, their absence reduces transparency about the skill's capabilities and restrictions.
findings.evidence: YAML frontmatter only contains name and description. Missing: allowed-tools, license, compatibility.
findings.remediation: Add allowed-tools, license, and compatibility fields to improve transparency.
